title: Dependency Upgrade Ladder Loop
summary: Upgrades dependencies through safe rungs instead of one large risky jump.
steps: Read release notes and breaking changes for each version interval that matters.
Define ladder rungs that minimize simultaneous behavior changes.
Upgrade one rung at a time, run compatibility gates, and commit only green rungs.
When a rung fails, isolate whether the break comes from API, configuration, runtime, or transitive dependencies.
Document any migration decisions so future upgrades have a map.
checks: build and tests pass at each accepted rung
stopConditions: blocked by incompatible dependency or product decision
budget ends after preserving last green rung
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Dependency Upgrade Ladder Loop for [dependency, target_version, compatibility_gates].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Read release notes and breaking changes for each version interval that matters.
- Define ladder rungs that minimize simultaneous behavior changes.
- Upgrade one rung at a time, run compatibility gates, and commit only green rungs.
- When a rung fails, isolate whether the break comes from API, configuration, runtime, or transitive dependencies.
- Document any migration decisions so future upgrades have a map.

Quality gates before completion:
- lockfile changes are explained
- build and tests pass at each accepted rung
- breaking changes are handled deliberately
- rollback path is clear

Stop when one of these is true:
- target version reached
- blocked by incompatible dependency or product decision
- budget ends after preserving last green rung

Finish with:
- version ladder
- release note links
- test output per rung
- rollback note

Escalate for human approval before: major runtime version changes, license changes, large public API migration.
